behavior_summary: This script implements the per-tick/process logic for a persistent fire/flame item: it advances the item's animation frame, and on particular frames/ticks attempts to spawn temporary subsidiary effects (lava pools and pyro sparks) at offsets from the item's position.

Before creating effects it computes explicit X,Y,Z candidate positions from the host item's coordinates and validates placement with Item::legal_create; only on success does it spawn LAVA or PYROS objects.
It uses the FREE subsystem (spawned helper FREE::1C19 and calls to FREE::2A56) around spawn points to schedule or perform cleanup of the temporary effects.
The routine loops/suspends to repeat this behavior over time while the fire item persists; frame/state bytes and small local flags gate when and which effects are produced.
notable_control_flow: Read current frame (Item::getFrame) into a local byte (BP-02) and branch behavior based on comparisons against constants (examples: 0x1D, 0x23, 0x0F, 0x2C).
On specific frame values, compute two distinct sets of candidate offsets (two X/Y/Z triplets) from Item::getX/Y/Z and run Item::legal_create for each candidate.
If Item::legal_create returns success, spawn either LAVA (shape 0x6F) or PYROS (shape 0x6D) at the returned slot/address; otherwise skip that spawn path.
Call/Spawn FREE helpers: spawn FREE::1C19 (passed pid and a small flag) frequently before suspend/around spawn points; in some branches call FREE::2A56 prior to spawning PYROS::3E82.
Advance or set the item's frame via Item::setFrame and loop/suspend so the script runs again on subsequent ticks.
open_questions: Precise semantic meaning of FREE::1C19 and FREE::2A56 (appear to schedule or perform cleanup/freeing for temporary effects, but exact behavior is inferred).
Exact identity and gameplay behavior of shapes 0x6D and 0x6F (mapped to PYROS/LAVA here by pattern; their in-game lifetimes/effects are not fully specified).
Meaning of small local byte flags (BP-0Ah/BP-0Bh etc.): whether they represent cooldowns, one-shot guards, or state counters is inferred but not certain.
Why some branches call FREE::2A56 (a call) before spawning PYROS::3E82 while others do not — the side effects expected from FREE::2A56 are not explicitly documented.

main_function.name: U8P_FIRE::0080
main_function.purpose: Drive the persistent fire item: advance animation frames, periodically attempt to spawn nearby temporary lava and pyro effects after validating placement, and arrange cleanup via FREE helper routines.
entry_conditions: Runs as the active/process script for a fire item (invoked repeatedly, suspending and looping each tick).
Triggered by the item's lifecycle/engine tick rather than a one-off external call.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRE::0080
function_map.role: Per-tick controller for a fire/flame item that drives animation and spawns temporary effects.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Read current animation/frame byte from the item to decide behavior and timing.

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Return item X coordinate used to compute spawn X offsets.

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Return item Y coordinate used to compute spawn Y offsets.

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Return item Z coordinate used to compute spawn Z/height offsets.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Advance or set the item's animation frame to step its visual/state progression.

function_map.name: Item::legal_create
function_map.role: Validate whether a shape can be created at a candidate X,Y,Z; returns an output slot/address used by spawn calls.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawned helper used around spawn/suspend points; inferred to schedule timed cleanup/freeing for transient effects.

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: Called (not spawned) in some branches before creating PYROS::3E82; inferred to register or perform immediate cleanup/registration for spawned objects.

function_map.name: LAVA::00AF
function_map.role: Spawn handler that creates a temporary lava effect at a validated spawn slot/address.

function_map.name: PYROS::20A1
function_map.role: Spawn handler that creates a pyro/spark effect at a validated spawn slot/address.

function_map.name: PYROS::3E82
function_map.role: Spawn handler that creates a different variant of pyro/ember effect, typically after FREE::2A56 is called.

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the current animation/state frame to drive branching and timing.
Retrieve base X coordinate used to compute spawn X offsets for candidate positions.
Retrieve base Y coordinate used to compute spawn Y offsets for candidate positions.
Retrieve base Z coordinate used to compute spawn Z offsets/height for placement checks.
Validate placement of a shape at computed offsets; produces an output slot/address used by the subsequent spawn if allowed.
Spawn a temporary lava object at a location validated by Item::legal_create.
Spawn a pyro/spark visual at a validated location.
Spawn an alternate pyro-related visual, typically after calling FREE::2A56.
Spawned repeatedly around suspend/spawn points; likely schedules or marks timed cleanup/free operations for temporary effects.
Called in some branches before spawning certain pyros; inferred to perform immediate registration/cleanup actions for the upcoming spawn.
Update the item's frame to the next animation/state value so behavior evolves across ticks.

reads.name: pid
reads.likely_effect: Read and passed as the owner/process id to FREE::1C19 spawns (used to scope spawned helper/cleanup operations).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the per-tick/process logic for a persistent fire/flame item: it advances the item's animation frame, and on particular frames/ticks attempts to spawn temporary subsidiary effects (lava pools and pyro sparks) at offsets from the item's position.
